seo.title: Ad Profitability & Metrics Topic Hub | Ad Breakeven Blog
seo.description: ROAS vs ROI, ad profit after costs, and how to tell if campaigns are actually profitable — not just revenue-positive on platform dashboards.

headline: Ad Profitability & Metrics
intro: Revenue ROAS and account ROI answer different questions. This cluster helps you pick the right metric, calculate net ad profit after costs, and spot campaigns that look fine in-platform but lose money after margin.

blocks.text: A campaign can hit 4× revenue ROAS and still be unprofitable at 30% margin. Ad profit subtracts product costs and ad spend from revenue — the number that actually matters for scaling.
Compare actual ROAS to break-even ROAS, then translate the gap into dollars with an ad profit calculation.

faq.question: Can ROAS look good but still lose money?
faq.answer: Yes. Platform ROAS uses revenue, not contribution margin. High revenue at low margin often means ad spend exceeds profit kept per sale.

faq.question: What is the difference between ROAS and ROI?
faq.answer: ROAS is revenue divided by ad spend. ROI typically measures profit relative to spend or investment. ROAS is faster for ad dashboards; ROI reflects true return after costs.

faq.question: How do I check if a campaign is profitable?
faq.answer: Compare actual ROAS to break-even ROAS, or calculate net ad profit: revenue minus product costs minus ad spend.
